summary.leadingMessage: Multiple growth lanes are below threshold and need intervention before leadership widens the story.

items.thresholdTheme: Expand only while policy coverage, evaluation gates, and escalation ownership remain inside agreed board thresholds.
items.boardQuestion: Which flagship lane can still scale without widening governance risk?
items.currentPosture: The AI lane is already producing scorecards, policy packets, and operating proofs, so expansion can continue if control coverage stays intact.
items.failureTrigger: Critical agent flows ship without evaluation or policy-bound approval steps.
items.downsideTrigger: Prompt or procurement exceptions grow faster than governance staffing can absorb.
items.headline: AI can keep advancing while governance thresholds remain visible and staffed.
items.narrative: This lane is not blocked by ambition; it is gated by whether the control plane can continue absorbing more agent activity without widening exception drag.
items.nextMove: Advance the next agent tranche only after re-confirming approval coverage and eval ownership.

dependencyAssessment.message: Dependency coverage remains inside threshold for the current growth posture.

operatingAssessment.message: Operating discipline supports the current growth motion without forcing a pause.

downsideAssessment.message: Downside containment remains credible for the current board story.

cashAssessment.message: Cash exposure remains inside the current risk tolerance.

confidenceAssessment.message: Board confidence is strong enough to support the current lane narrative.

items.thresholdTheme: Hold identity expansion until remediation debt, stale review evidence, and ownership gaps move back inside threshold.
items.boardQuestion: Which trust lane matters strategically but should not widen until hygiene recovers?
items.currentPosture: Identity surfaces exist and are useful, but the next scope increase would outpace evidence freshness and role-drift cleanup.
items.failureTrigger: Privileged review exceptions age beyond the defined committee tolerance.
items.downsideTrigger: More business units join the program before reviewer coverage stabilizes.
items.headline: Identity should hold at the current edge until remediation discipline improves.
items.narrative: Leadership can still present the identity story confidently, but not if the next scale step outruns review closure and exception ownership.
items.nextMove: Hold expansion and clear reviewer drift before widening policy scope.

dependencyAssessment.message: Dependency coverage is drifting toward threshold pressure and needs tighter board guardrails.

operatingAssessment.message: Operating discipline is below threshold and should block the next growth expansion.

downsideAssessment.message: Downside containment is below threshold and the board should intervene before scale widens exposure.

cashAssessment.message: Cash exposure is rising toward a board-level watch threshold.

confidenceAssessment.message: Board confidence is too weak to support the current growth claim.

items.thresholdTheme: Intervene when reporting dependence grows faster than attribution trust and release integrity.
items.boardQuestion: Which growth lane is most likely to overstate readiness if threshold guardrails are ignored?
items.currentPosture: Reporting, experimentation, and attribution layers are live, but confidence drops when delivery pressure outpaces data-quality fixes.
items.failureTrigger: Board reporting uses metrics sourced from unresolved attribution or refresh incidents.
items.downsideTrigger: Teams fund more experiments before the reporting base is trusted.
items.headline: Revenue systems need intervention thresholds, not a broader growth claim.
items.narrative: This lane can still support the board, but only if attribution drift and refresh failures trigger a visible pause before another big growth promise gets made.
items.nextMove: Intervene on reporting trust and refresh reliability before approving the next experiment-heavy budget.

dependencyAssessment.message: Dependency coverage is below threshold and should trigger immediate board-visible intervention.

operatingAssessment.message: Operating discipline is under pressure and could undermine the next growth claim.

downsideAssessment.message: Downside containment is below threshold and the board should intervene before scale widens exposure.

cashAssessment.message: Cash exposure is below the accepted threshold and should trigger a capital conversation.

confidenceAssessment.message: Board confidence is too weak to support the current growth claim.

items.thresholdTheme: Reallocate leadership attention when fragmented payment, KYC, and reserve proofs cross the downside threshold.
items.boardQuestion: Which lane should trigger capital reallocation because confidence is too fragmented?
items.currentPosture: Payments, merchant review, KYC, and treasury surfaces exist, but the operating case is still split across too many partial proofs.
items.failureTrigger: Deal teams cite merchant or treasury readiness without one consolidated diligence packet.
items.downsideTrigger: Exception queues widen while management keeps positioning the lane as scale-ready.
items.headline: FinTech should trigger reallocation before another scale narrative reaches investors.
items.narrative: The right move is not more packaging. It is leadership reallocation into the broken thresholds that still separate payments, merchant risk, and KYC into fragmented narratives.
items.nextMove: Reallocate committee attention to one integrated diligence threshold brief.

dependencyAssessment.message: Dependency coverage is below threshold and should trigger immediate board-visible intervention.

operatingAssessment.message: Operating discipline is below threshold and should block the next growth expansion.

downsideAssessment.message: Downside containment is below threshold and the board should intervene before scale widens exposure.

cashAssessment.message: Cash exposure is below the accepted threshold and should trigger a capital conversation.

confidenceAssessment.message: Board confidence is too weak to support the current growth claim.

items.thresholdTheme: Advance the buyer-proof lane while questionnaire cycle time and trust evidence remain inside threshold.
items.boardQuestion: Which commercial infrastructure lane is strongest enough to accelerate now?
items.currentPosture: The procurement surfaces are reusable, buyer-readable, and already organized into an answerable trust layer.
items.failureTrigger: Questionnaire response time stretches beyond the committed buyer window.
items.downsideTrigger: Trust assets drift out of date while the commercial team scales outreach.
items.headline: Procurement is inside threshold and can safely support more enterprise demand.
items.narrative: This is the cleanest commercial lane because the evidence room, answer studio, and proof monitoring already reduce buyer friction instead of creating more it.
items.nextMove: Advance enterprise outreach while preserving evidence freshness and response SLA discipline.

dependencyAssessment.message: Dependency coverage remains inside threshold for the current growth posture.

operatingAssessment.message: Operating discipline supports the current growth motion without forcing a pause.

downsideAssessment.message: Downside containment remains credible for the current board story.

cashAssessment.message: Cash exposure remains inside the current risk tolerance.

confidenceAssessment.message: Board confidence is strong enough to support the current lane narrative.

items.thresholdTheme: Hold regulated growth claims until evidence continuity and review staffing prove they can absorb the next tranche.
items.boardQuestion: Which regulated lane should stay visible but not yet expand?
items.currentPosture: Assay, instrument, CAPA, specimen, and safety surfaces are live, but regulated-team continuity remains the gating factor.
items.failureTrigger: Change control, chain of custody, or signal review packets go stale between audit cycles.
items.downsideTrigger: More scope lands before review staffing and reuse patterns stabilize.
items.headline: Biotech should hold at the current footprint until review continuity is stronger.
items.narrative: The vertical is real and buyer-legible, but leadership should not oversell scale until the regulated review system proves it can absorb more lanes cleanly.
items.nextMove: Hold expansion and strengthen review continuity before marketing the next regulated tranche.

dependencyAssessment.message: Dependency coverage is drifting toward threshold pressure and needs tighter board guardrails.

operatingAssessment.message: Operating discipline is under pressure and could undermine the next growth claim.

downsideAssessment.message: Downside containment is thinning and needs visible triggers before expansion continues.

cashAssessment.message: Cash exposure is rising toward a board-level watch threshold.

confidenceAssessment.message: Board confidence is fragile and depends on a tighter threshold packet.
